footer.headline: A simpler waiting-room stack for clinics that need order now
footer.deck: The suite connects kiosk, waiting room display, reception, web booking, and mobile follow-up without demanding enterprise overhead.

columns.title: Integrations
lines: QR, thermal printer, and browser-based TV display
Web booking, webhooks, and queue API

landing.title: Clinic flow suite | kiosk + display + web
landing.description: Operations software for small and medium clinics with kiosk, waiting room display, reception console, and web booking connected together.
landing.heading: Clinic flow suite with kiosk, waiting room display, reception, and web booking

hero.title: Bring order to the waiting room and clarity to the whole visit.
hero.deck: The suite joins kiosk, calling display, reception console, and web booking into one operational reading. Patients know where to enter, staff know what comes next, and management finally gets something measurable.

metrics.label: connected modules
metrics.detail: Kiosk, waiting room display, reception, and web booking on one state model.

metrics.label: initial rollout
metrics.detail: A light pilot setup for one site without enterprise-grade overhead.

metrics.label: ideal sites
metrics.detail: Built for growing clinics, not for hospital-scale enterprise complexity.

panels.title: Scheduled and walk-in check-in
panels.copy: Patients enter the same queue whether they arrive from web, QR, or front desk.

panels.title: Privacy-aware live calls
panels.copy: Large display with ticket code, initials, and consult room.

panels.title: Call, reprint, close, measure
panels.copy: Station-safe operations for real consult rooms and active staff.

panels.title: Booking and reminders tied to queue state
panels.copy: Web, QR, WhatsApp, and queue status under one operational story.

modules.title: This is not a standalone kiosk. It is a lightweight operating layer for the whole waiting-room experience.
modules.deck: Each module has a clear role, but all of them share the same state so patient, reception, and leadership work from the same truth.

cards.title: Guided in-room check-in
cards.copy: For scheduled and walk-in patients, with quick help, large text mode, and one-tap receptionist support.

cards.title: Privacy-aware live calling
cards.copy: The screen shows ticket, initials, and consult room so the room understands what is happening without exposing sensitive data.

cards.title: Queue operations for real consult rooms
cards.copy: Call, reprint, complete, flag no-show, and control stations from a console built for active front desk teams.

cards.title: Web booking and reminders connected to the queue
cards.copy: The website stops being a separate brochure and becomes the digital entry point of the same clinic flow.

journeys.title: Different audiences enter at different moments, but the system never splits.
journeys.deck: Patients, reception, and management read different stages of the same flow. That continuity is what makes the suite feel like a real product.

lanes.title: Confirms from QR or phone and enters the queue early
steps: Receives a WhatsApp or SMS reminder.
Opens the link, confirms arrival, and sees estimated wait time.
If they still use the kiosk on arrival, the same queue state is already there.

lanes.title: Ordered intake without flooding front desk with repeated questions
steps: The kiosk creates a quick ticket with initials and visible help options.
The TV shows who is being called without exposing the full name or phone number.
Reception can reprint or adjust the ticket without rebuilding the case.

lanes.title: Reads wait time, no-show, and consult room load
steps: A dashboard summarizes served tickets, SLA, and no-show.
The site sees peak windows and queue bottlenecks.
Operations improve without pretending to replace a full EMR.

surfaces.title: Four moments, one product story
surfaces.deck: The landing frames the promise. Mobile check-in makes it tangible. Queue status removes friction. The dashboard gives the budget owner a reason to sign.

cards.title: Patients confirm from QR or direct link
cards.copy: A simple flow for arrival confirmation, scheduled vs walk-in intake, and fast handoff to queue status.

mockup.title: Check-in ready
mockup.footer: The same queue continues in-room and at reception.

cards.title: Patients can follow progress from the phone
cards.copy: The waiting room display is useful for the room. The phone view is useful for the person who is actually waiting.

mockup.title: Site queue
mockup.footer: Ideal for QR on reminders, lobby, or printed ticket.

cards.title: The owner sees the KPI story behind renewal
cards.copy: The deal no longer lives only at front desk level. Management can see wait time, no-show, served volume, and consult room load.

mockup.title: Daily summary
mockup.footer: A site view first, a small-chain view next.

integrations.title: The integration story should read just as clearly
integrations.deck: Buyers need to understand in minutes what enters the pilot, what connects on day one, and what can scale later without rebuilding the base.

pricing.title: Clear packages that sell value before complexity
pricing.deck: The decision should feel simple: start with waiting-room order, add patient visibility, and close with management insight.

plans.name: Room Base
plans.fit: Small practices that need waiting room order and front desk relief.

plans.name: Flow Plus
plans.fit: Clinics with active booking, reminders, and a need for patient visibility.
features: Mobile check-in by QR or direct link

plans.name: Lite Multi-site
plans.fit: Growing small chains that need branding, roles, and site KPI visibility.

security.title: Visible privacy and dependable operations from day one
security.deck: Trust does not come from an abstract technical promise. It comes from better screens, clearer roles, and continuity when the room is under pressure.

cards.title: Privacy-safe display
items: TV shows ticket, initials, and consult room
No full phone number or full patient name outside admin
Clear state for staff, patient, and room

cards.title: Lite roles, branding, and sites
items: Reception, admin, and management with separate access
Basic branding per clinic or small chain
Prepared for 1 to 5 sites without enterprise overhead

cards.title: Operational continuity
items: Offline outbox in kiosk
TV snapshot and visible reconnection state
Reception can keep operating and reprint tickets

items.question: Do we need proprietary hardware to launch?
items.answer: No. The pilot is designed to work with a browser, a TV or monitor, a PC or tablet, and a compatible thermal printer.

items.question: Does this replace the clinic's full medical software?
items.answer: Not at this stage. The suite competes as waiting room operations + booking + reminders + reporting, not as a full EMR.

items.question: Can we start with a single site?
items.answer: Yes. The first model is built for a pilot site, then expands to branding, lite multi-site, and dashboard layers only if the flow proves itself.

items.question: How do you reduce no-show if patients never touch the kiosk?
items.answer: The improvement does not depend on the kiosk alone. It comes from reminders, mobile check-in, queue visibility, and a front desk that sees the same state.

finalCta.title: Walk the suite in the same order a buyer would
finalCta.deck: First the promise is clear. Then the check-in becomes real. Then queue status lowers uncertainty. Finally the dashboard justifies budget and renewal.

hero.title: Arrival should not start at the front desk.
hero.deck: This is where the suite becomes tangible: a patient confirms from QR, reminder, or link, enters the right flow, and removes one of the biggest pressure points from reception.

finalCta.title: Once arrival is resolved, patients need orientation
finalCta.deck: The next step is not another form. It is queue visibility: whether the call is close, how long it may take, and where to go.

hero.title: Waiting stops feeling opaque.
hero.deck: The room display informs the room. The private phone view informs the person who is actually waiting. That shift lowers anxiety, cuts repeated questions, and makes the experience feel cared for.

finalCta.title: Now the suite reads like a complete product
finalCta.deck: A clear promise, a smoother arrival, visible waiting, and management insight. That continuity is what closes more of the gap.
